title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard introduced by Google Chrome that enables AI agents to interact with websites through structured tools instead of DOM automation. It provides both declarative APIs using HTML attributes and imperative APIs for custom JavaScript tools, allowing agents to call named typed actions directly rather than relying on pixel-based clicking and layout interpretation.
writeup: This talk introduces WebMCP, a web standard proposed by Google Chrome that lets AI agents interact with websites through structured, named tools rather than DOM automation. It matters because it replaces pixel-based clicking and layout interpretation, which is brittle, with typed actions that a site explicitly exposes. The concrete takeaways: WebMCP offers a declarative API built on HTML attributes and an imperative API for custom JavaScript tools, and agents call these named typed actions directly.
